source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Consider the circuit shown in the <image>. In this circuit \( R = 1 \, \text{k}\Omega \) and \( C = 1 \, \mu F \). The input voltage is sinusoidal with a frequency of 50 Hz, represented as a phasor with magnitude \( V_i \) and phase angle 0 radian as shown in the <image>. The output voltage is represented as a phasor with magnitude \( V_o \) and phase angle \( \delta \) radian. What is the value of the output phase angle \( \delta \) (in radian) relative to the phase angle of the input voltage?
extraction_notes: Removed leading hint/instruction text from the question.
Extracted choices from the problem text and mapped solution letter D to its option text.
Image field contains a PIL image placeholder rather than a path/URL, so no image path was extracted.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'Consider the circuit shown in the image'
the circuit topology needed to determine phase angle is not specified in text alone
multi_step: requires identifying the circuit from the image before determining the phase relationship
given R, C, frequency, and phasor framing, solving likely needs circuit recognition plus electrical reasoning/calculation
multiple-choice with 4 options suggests a single final target but not purely one-step lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and a single explicit target value is provided
choices provide a closed set of candidate phase angles

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
asset registry passed with no recorded issues or risk flags
image dependency is very high, making this a strong image-grounded candidate
verifiability is high because the target answer is explicit and short-text
